predictions: Harry: One major public SaaS CEO will do Elon Musk layoff—cut 50% headcount in one day, shock the market
Jason: Claude Code will aggressively attack Figma, Replit, Lovable this year; only defensible if they own entire value chain
Harry: PE-backed 6%+ growth SaaS companies forced into draconian headcount cuts to service debt
Jason: Citrini's ghost GDP thesis overstated on timeline but embedded truth: 4-5M job displacement if tech headcount cut 50%
disagreements: Yes—Jason emphasizes pace acceleration (things move faster than expected); Rory pushes back on extrapolation, argues macro takes longer to play out, warns against dismissing 10-year productivity cycle benefits. Harry mostly agrees with Jason on urgency.
harry_role: Most bearish of trio, driving consolidation + ghost GDP theses, leveraging Jason's on-the-ground startup data as cover fire, somewhat dismissive of Rory's long-term productivity defense.
notable: Ghost GDP concept crystallized: Jason's real example (12→3 people, same revenue) plus Claude's modeling of 50% tech headcount cut = $600-900B GDP impact, 4-5M jobs lost—unprecedented shock outside war/pandemic.
